free_text: Deuteron-induced reactions on Ni isotopes up to 60 MeV
U-120M variable energy cyclotron
Beam energy was determined with accuracy of 1% with
full width at half maximum (FWHM) of 1.8%.
High-purity Ni foils (Goodfellow, 99.9% purity, 25 um
declared thickness) and Al foils (50 um declared
thickness) were weighed within 2% of accuracy.
50% efficiency, resolution of 1.8 keV FWHM at
1.3 MeV.
External deuteron beam
Flux was determined by a Faraday cup. Monitor
reactions served only for verification.
Ni foils interleaved with Al foils that were
used for additional monitoring of the beam
current and deuteron energy loss.
The mean energy, energy thickness, and energy
spread in each foil were simulated by the SRIM
Cooling time of less than 100 days.
Run  Init.Ed  Total charge  Irrad.time  Mean current
No.   (MeV)      (uC)          (sec)        (uA)
Energy thickness of each foil and initial
energy spread (1.8% FWHM) and error (1%)
Total uncertainty from
- Statistics in activity determination
- Charge measurement                    (~5%)
- Foil thickness                         (2%)
- Detector efficiency determination      (2%)
Table III of Phys.Rev.C94(2016)014606
Approved by Eva Simeckova (2016-08-02)
On
By author:
The time dependence of activity and the relative
intensities of 67.412 keV (85%), 908.631 keV (3.6%),
841.21 keV (0.79%) are consistent with 61Cu decay.
The upper limit for the Ni(d,x)61Co reaction is in
the order of present experimental errors and thus
the contribution of 61Co decay to the observed
activity is not significant below 20 MeV.
The uncertainty 97.9 mb printed in Table III of
Phys.Rev.C94(2016)014606 should read 9.8 mb.
(Eva Simeckova, 2016-07-28)
By author:
Measured after a period of time of ~10*T1/2(58mCo).
58gCo 810.8 keV gamma-line activity was
decomposed to metastable and ground state components
by fitting to the Bateman equation.
The results were consistent (within 10%) with a direct
measurement of 24.9 keV gamma line corrected for
intensity attenuation in the measured foil.
By author:
Measured after more than 2 weeks, and thus include
contribution of 57Ni decay to 57Co.
